title: Handling Data Validation Errors
text: Handling Data Validation Errors When the validation process fails, ProcScript functions provide information about the failure. When an error occurs: Status contains a negative value $procerror gives the detailed reason $procerrorcontext identifies exactly where the error occurred in ProcScript $dataerrorcontext identifies the data context where the error occurred If declarative or procedural validation fails, Uniface activates the field-level or entity-level error trigger as appropriate for each occurrence, and sets $error to the relevant value to indicate why the validation process has failed. $error is set to: 0140 for the field-level validate trigger 0147 for the validateKey or entity-level validate trigger After validation is completed, $status , $procerror , and $dataerrorcontext are set to reflect the first validation error encountered by the validate statement. These functions therefore provide different values during the validation process: During the validation process, $status , $procerror , $dataerrorcontext , and $procerrorcontext return values for the current validation error. After validation is completed, $status , $procerror and $dataerrorcontext return values for the first error encountered in the validation process. For example, when validation of an occurrence is requested, Uniface first attempts to validate the first field of the primary key. If validation fails for the first field of the primary key, Uniface activates the error trigger for that field. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
